note: This is a template showing the expected structure for watch_out research output. Each route should have 4-6 specific, actionable hazard entries.

routes.name: Czech it Out
watch_out: Loose blocky terrain on approach and first pitch; dislodge carefully and be alert for rockfall from other parties above
Crumbly rock quality with 25+ feet of runout between protection points on pitch 1; requires solid footwork and confidence on 5.8 climbing
Afternoon thunderstorms common July-August; exposed to weather on upper pitches; start climbing by 7am for safe descent before 2pm
Descent involves downclimbing and route-finding; multiple gully options in area; cairns unreliable; practice descent route mentally before climbing
Snow/ice possible through June on adjacent gullies; may require 4th class scrambling to bypass approach hazards

routes.name: Freedom or Death
watch_out: Washington Pass avalanche slope exposure on approach; assess snow stability carefully April-June; generally safe July-September
Runout 5.10 slab pitch (pitch 3) with 30+ feet exposure; loose rock and poor protection quality; requires excellent footwork
Mixed terrain transitions (5.7 rock to ice) on pitches 4-5 have limited belay options; ice quality variable depending on season
Descent route-finding critical; wrong turns lead into wrong gully systems; study descent route on guidebook/photos before climb
Rockfall hazard mid-afternoon as sun melts frozen rock; popular route with crowding; stay alert and position defensively; start early
Weather deteriorates rapidly after 1pm; afternoon thunderstorms common June-August; morning-only summits recommended

routes.name: Northwest Face (Boving-Pollock)
watch_out: Upper pitches have sparse protection quality; 5.11 climbing with 20-30 feet between marginal pieces; requires confidence and good placing
Rappel descent requires 2-3 rappels with marginal anchors; fixed slings often weathered and worn; carry personal anchor material; verify all systems
Snow moat guards base of route through mid-July; may require 4th class climbing or short ice climbing (WI2) to bypass; ice screws recommended
Exposed climbing throughout; northwest face gets afternoon weather; afternoon thunderstorms possible mid-July through August; early start essential
Route-finding on descent complex; multiple anchor stations and options; practice rappel sequences before deploying on route

routes.name: South Face
watch_out: Weather exposure on south-facing wall; afternoon sun and thermal winds; afternoon thunderstorms July-August; start by 7am
Route-finding on descent important; multiple gully options and ridges; cairns unreliable; study descent before climbing
Loose rock quality in sections; be careful dislodging blocks and stay alert for rockfall from other parties
Snow/ice possible upper pitches through June; ice axe and crampons recommended for season safety
Commitment level moderate; 6-8 hours round trip including descent; turnaround time essential for darkness buffer

routes.name: Beckey-Davis
watch_out: Mixed terrain (5.7 rock to 3rd class scrambling) with variable belay quality; some pitches lack adequate belay anchors
Loose rock common throughout; careful footwork and block testing required; stay alert for rockfall from other parties
Altitude and exertion; Prusik Peak ascent from Colchuck Lake base camp is high and committing; proper acclimatization and fitness important
Weather exposure; west-facing terrain receives afternoon storms; morning ascents strongly recommended; weather can deteriorate rapidly
Descent complexity; route-finding on descent as important as ascent; practice route mentally and verify cairn system

routes.name: Upper North Ridge w/Great Gendarme
watch_out: North Face avalanche slope exposure visible from route; assess snow stability carefully April-July; generally safe August-September
Great Gendarme crux (5.9 mixed) has exposure and sparse protection; requires solid mixed terrain technique; fall zone significant
Complex descent route-finding; many party reversals near summit area; route options and junctions require careful navigation
Long commitment; 8-10 hours round trip from base; turnaround time critical; conservative descent decision-making essential
Afternoon thunderstorms common August; weather deteriorates rapidly after 1pm; morning-only summit ascents recommended

instructions: Replace sample entries above with actual research data for all 95 priority routes. Each route must include: id, name, mountainId (area), grade, discipline, and watch_out array with 4-6 specific hazard entries. Validate output using validate-watch-out.js before integration.
